steps.title: Close the title sentence
steps.content: The first clause says Hampl's essay is included in Home Ground: A Guide to the American Landscape. The following words, Edited by Barry Lopez and Debra Gwartney, introduce a new sentence about the book.

steps.title: Use the participial opener
steps.content: A would join two independent clauses with only a comma. B creates a nonparallel phrase, and C fuses edited directly to the title while still leaving a comma splice later. A period cleanly ends the title sentence before the introductory participial phrase. The answer is D.
